description_long: This object is a vintage, portable control cabinet or data recorder, featuring a robust rectangular metal housing with a dark blue and black finish. The front panel is equipped with a large roll of graph paper, multiple rotary knobs, switches, and indicator lights, suggesting it is used for monitoring or recording electrical or mechanical data. A sturdy handle is mounted on the top for easy transport, and the sides are plain with only a few protruding connectors and fasteners. The back panel is mostly featureless except for some small ports and faint labeling, indicating manufacturer or model information. The overall construction appears industrial, with a focus on durability and functionality, and the visible components are primarily metallic with some plastic and rubber elements. The device is likely used in technical or engineering environments for data acquisition or control purposes.
description: A vintage, portable control cabinet with a metal housing, front panel controls, and a large graph paper roll for data recording. It features a top handle and industrial design elements.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main control interface, including the graph paper roll, rotary knobs, switches, and indicator lights.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the plain metal side with a few protruding connectors and the top handle visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a mostly featureless back panel with faint labeling and a few small ports.
description_view_3: The right side view shows another plain metal side with minimal features and the top handle.
